quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! Their wings can rotate in a full circle, allowing them to hover in place and even fly upside down for short distances. The Pacific Northwest is home to the beautiful Rufous Hummingbird, which migrates through Port Angeles each summer.

short_read.title: How Hummingbirds Fly in the Rain
short_read.teaser: These tiny birds are tougher than they look, able to fly through rainstorms that would ground larger birds. Scientists have discovered their secret involves shaking water off their heads in milliseconds.

medium_read.title: The Gardens of Budapest: A Journey Through Hungarian Horticulture
medium_read.teaser: From the historic Buda Castle gardens to the beloved Margaret Island, Budapest's green spaces tell stories of royal gardeners and centuries of cultivation. These gardens blend Hungarian tradition with plants from around the world.

long_read.title: The Secret Lives of Olympic National Park's Ancient Trees
long_read.teaser: In the Hoh Rainforest just hours from Port Angeles, scientists are discovering that trees communicate with each other through underground networks. These thousand-year-old giants hold secrets about cooperation, resilience, and the wisdom of forests.

port_angeles.title: Crabbing Season on the Strait
port_angeles.description: July is prime time for Dungeness crab along the Strait of Juan de Fuca! You can often watch locals checking their crab pots from the Port Angeles City Pier, and the fresh catch shows up at local restaurants and markets. The Dungeness crab is named after the small town of Dungeness, just east of Port Angeles.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart can beat up to ___ times per minute during flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's exactly right! Isn't it amazing how fast such a tiny heart can beat?
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is 1,260 beats per minute — incredibly fast!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: The answer is 1,260 beats per minute! That's more than 20 times per second — incredible for such a small bird!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: By comparison, a human heart at rest beats about 60-100 times per minute. When hummingbirds are resting at night, their heart rate can slow to as few as 50 beats per minute to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is indeed the Hungarian word for butterfly — such a lovely, musical word!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is pillangó! Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee. Pillangó is such a beautiful word for butterfly!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is unique among European languages, belonging to the Finno-Ugric language family rather than the Indo-European family. Many Hungarian words have a melodic quality that reflects the country's rich musical heritage.

puzzle_3.question: What is the deepest part of the Strait of Juan de Fuca?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Excellent! At 650 feet deep, the Strait is deep enough to host whales, submarine canyons, and all sorts of fascinating marine life!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: The deepest part is actually 650 feet! That's deeper than a 60-story building is tall, providing a highway for gray whales and orcas traveling through.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca connects the Pacific Ocean to Puget Sound and was named after a Greek navigator who sailed for Spain. Its deep channels and strong currents create rich feeding grounds for marine mammals and seabirds.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
